name: Mom's German Meatballs
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the garlic into a water glass and pour 3 tablespoons of boiling water over it. Mash the garlic well, then add another 3 tablespoons of boiling water. Strain through a kitchen towel, saving the liquid only.
2. Mix together all the ingredients, including the garlic liquid, in a large bowl.
3. Shape the mixture into balls about the size of a large egg.
4. Roll the meatballs in flour until well coated.
5. Melt enough fat to cover the bottom of a heavy skillet or cast iron pot.
6. Brown the meatballs in the fat.
7. Remove the meatballs and pour off all but 3 tablespoons of the fat.
8. In a saucepan, add 1 quart of water and the reserved catsup. Make a paste with the remaining ingredients (sauce components) and mix into enough water to achieve a thin cream consistency.
9. Once the water-catsup mixture is warm, slowly add it to the pan, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Allow the sauce to thicken slightly.
10. Place the meatballs in the pan or an oven-proof roaster, ensuring they are covered with the sauce.
11. Cover the pan and bake in a hot oven for 1 hour.
12. Serve with mashed potatoes (or pasta or rice). Enjoy!
